title: Mode switch commands require exact string match — natural language ignored
what_failed: 'switch to normie mode' did not trigger mode switch. Claude responded as if it switched but self.mode was unchanged. Only exact 'normie mode' worked.
where_failed: pi_agent.py process_input() — 'if user_input.lower() == "normie mode"' exact equality check.
why_likely: String equality instead of flexible matching. Users naturally say 'switch to X mode' or 'go normie'.
reproduction: Type 'switch to normie mode' instead of 'normie mode'. Pi (Claude in root mode) responds as if switched but mode is unchanged.
expected: Common natural variants trigger the mode switch.
actual: Only exact 'normie mode' / 'root mode' strings work.
suggested_fix: Change equality check to set membership: cmd in ('normie mode', 'switch to normie mode', 'go normie').
